Exterior.text: Overall Typical "Cub" Yellow with Black Accents in Traditional Cub Scheme

History.text: Built in 2001 and flown little. Sold into a Southern California desert hangared collection and not flown much. Then sold into a Bakersfield multi plane owner collection and not flown much. Then traded to Skywagons LLC for another plane. This Wag Aero Acro-Cub is literally like a 160 HP, solo-from-the-back, J3 with electric start and clipped wings with a fully aerobatic capability. And it is like new throughout. Complete logs. No damage history. Almost a new plane.

items: Cream with Vermilion and Gold Trim
Exterior.text: Cream with Vermilion and Gold Trim

Comments.text: Step back in time with this beautifully restored 1933 Waco UIC, a true classic of the Golden Age of Aviation. With its elegant lines, open cockpit charm, upgraded avionics, this rare Continental W670 powered biplane offers an unforgettable flying experience. With notable owners such as John Paul Riddle, Ruth Ellen Dailey Helm, Captain Carl Overstreet, and Maurice Hodges. Own a piece of history that turns heads on the ramp and captures hearts in the sky. Please visit https://thehangar.aircraftbidder.com/ for more information.

Pull handles and footstep for easy in and out access to cockpit Over the shoulder and lap safety belts Spinner to tail warranty

Sting S4 Design Features/Safety.text: The Sting S4 is an all-composite technology lending to a strong and light airframe that exceeds structural standards. The use of modern CAD/CAM technology is used throughout for an airframe that contains 85% carbon for maximum strength and weight savings. The wing skin and fuselage is a sandwich of carbon and foam core, glued together with epoxy glue. The coating is then evacuated and cured in an oven. Highly stressed parts are made in an autoclave high safety carbon frame with ROPS ensure crew safety, even in the case of the aircraft being overturned. Ballistic parachute recovery system installed in each aircraft as standard equipment.

items: You can order your aircraft with leather or fabric heated seats
Your interior is complete with smooth finishes to give it that polished appearance, which will consist of leather side walls and leather covered glareshield
You have different options for color choices for your interior package

title: 2025 TL Ultralight Sparker

Airframe & Power Systems Information.text: Aircraft Configuration / Use Light Sport Aircraft Props Power Max Three Blade Hydraulic Inflight Adjustable Propeller

items: Flight Rules: VFR or IFR Equipped
Maintenance Condition.text: Flight Rules: VFR or IFR Equipped

Engine.text: The Sparker can be ordered with different engine options: Rotax 912ULS, Rotax 915iS and the new 916iS for incredible performance and efficiency.

Avionics/Radios/Equipment.text: Full Garmin glass panel with 3 10” G3X displays, Garmin GMC 507 autopilot, Garmin 245R ADSB in and out, Garmin radios, and an option for IFR flight with the Garmin GTN 650 avionics package. An AOA indicator is also incorporated into the G3X system for added warning against stalls.

Additional Equipment.text: Internal USB power for charging personal devices. And an Iphone or Mini Ipad holder that slides in and out of the dash to allow for up to 5 screens in the cockpit. Luxury heated leather seats and in cabin heat.

Sparker Design Features/Safety.text: Hydraulic retractable landing gear.Trailing link main gear for robust performance on tarmac and grass fields. Carbon composite skin technology with Kevlar encased cockpit for added safety. Ergonomic side-stick controls with electronic aileron and elevator trim. The Sparker offers the Stratos Magnum 601 ballistic recovery chute. The design of the Sparker also incorporates a Kevlar wrapped cockpit. TL has engineered the Stream and the Sparker to have crumple zones not dissimilar to an automobile. The Kevlar keeps the carbon from shattering and the crumple zones help to absorb impact in the unfortunate event of a collision.

Performance/Efficiency.text: Wide and versatile flight envelope. Cruise speeds of 165-175 kts (916iS) with a stall speed of 44 kts and up to 2000 fpm climb. This wide envelope is due in part to the electric slotted fowler flaps which prove very effective at slowing the aircraft down. Incredible efficiency with an estimated 7 gph fuel burn at cruise. The Sparker was designed with efficiency in mind. The Rotax gives an expected range of 900 to1000 miles depending on the power plant.

Exterior/Interior.text: The largest cabin in the UL/LSA category with a 49.5 in. wide cabin that allows for greater comfort even with two larger passengers. It can also accommodate up to a 6’6” pilots. Electric adjustable pedals make it easy to find the perfect fit.

items: Baggage Compartment: Two Luggage Compartments with Exterior Luggage Doors. Front is .5 Cubic Feet and the Rear is 24 Cubic Feet
To put Things into Perspective, it can even Accommodate Two Golf Bags
Interior.text: Number of Seats: 2 Baggage Compartment: Two Luggage Compartments with Exterior Luggage Doors. Front is .5 Cubic Feet and the Rear is 24 Cubic Feet To put Things into Perspective, it can even Accommodate Two Golf Bags

Weight and Balance.text: Increased MTOW to 1653 lbs or 750kg. With this new weight limit you will have a no compromise weight and balance sheet. You can accommodate up to two 220lb pilots, full fuel (36 gallons), and the max luggage allowance. An empty weight of around 910 lbs.

Comments.text: TL spent over three years developing the Sparker. Breaking new ground in luxury and performance. The Sparker is the culmination of over 30 years of design excellence. The latest technology in CAD and 3D modeling was utilized to create a truly luxurious platform with amazing visibility and dynamic flight characteristics. Combining these features, and amenities with the latest in avionics, TL created an unparalleled aircraft that satisfies pilots who want an immersive and exhilarating flight experience in a roomy and relaxed cockpit. The Sparker is available for order today! Contact us now to schedule a demo flight in a Sparker with the Rotax 915iS.

Contact Information.text: Please tell the seller that you saw this ad on ASO Tel: (804) 400-8008 P.O. Box 763 Quinton, VA 23141 All Specifications Subject to Verification Upon Inspection Availability Subject to Prior Lease, Sale, or Withdrawal From Market Without Notice

No details have been overlooked, to make this a reliable time machine. Fly comfortably knowing how much time and efforts have been applied to the aircraft. description:
This aircraft has undergone an extensive three year period of maintenance and restoration, that includes new engine frame structure, canted bulkheads, spar inspection panels, engine beam replacement, new windshields, new ice boots, upgraded avionics, new exhaust, turbos, overhauled engines, upgraded American Aviation intercoolers, A/C system fixes, overhauled janitrol heater, overhauled props, updated interior, new skytech starters, new tires, and also is the only known fixed wing aircraft in North America with six Aspen screens!
All logbooks are complete and no known major damage history.  This is truly an example of an aircraft that has had many sets of scrutinizing eyes on it over the course of restoration.  Rest assured that you will be flying a safe and comfortable plane that has room for the family and all of their belongings.  For the cost of a used Cirrus SR22 you can fly in pressurized comfort with the whole family!
Call us today to set up a demonstration of this one of a kind 414!  Located at Wayne County, Ohio (KBJJ)
